claim: The deterministic tension-shortlist tool shall never mutate any domain graph.json: its only outputs are a printed shortlist and an advisory recommendation, and every surfaced pair is a SUSPECT for AI/steward review, never a decided conflict.
why: The generative-audit tool performs the CONFRONT-adjacent first act of sight, but sight is not decision (R-ai-presents-not-decides). In the Go port the tension-shortlist capability is realized as `hotam inspect` (internal/diagnose/inspect.go), which is structurally read-only by construction: a Candidate record (internal/diagnose/inspect.go) is ALWAYS advisory — it never blocks a write, never changes exit code (exit code always 0 regardless of how many candidates are found), and never decides anything. The recommendation text (advice function) explicitly invokes R-ai-presents-not-decides / R-decided-needs-human-signoff. If the sweep could write a Conflict node it would be deciding a tension exists rather than presenting a candidate; the steward must draft any ProposedConflict from a row. Keeping the tool read-only over the graph preserves the present-never-decide boundary at the generation organ. The historical Python tool (tools/audit_tensions.py) is Declared (not ported); its read-only property was proven by test_audit_never_writes_graph_py. The Go successor (hotam inspect) inherits the same read-only discipline by construction — it operates on an in-memory graph pointer for read only, with no write path. PROSE (not ENFORCED): the read-only property is upheld by code structure (inspect.go has no graph mutation calls), not by a machine enforcer test that asserts the absence of writes.
